name: FlashRank (Ultra-Light Re-Ranking)

auth.notes: Local library; model downloaded from HuggingFace Hub on first use

performance.notes: ONNX-optimized; 10-50x faster than full cross-encoder with similar quality

agent_readiness.notes: Ultra-fast cross-encoder re-ranking for RAG. from flashrank import Ranker, RerankRequest. ranker = Ranker(model_name='ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2'). req = RerankRequest(query='What is Python?', passages=[{'id': 1, 'text': 'Python is a programming language'}, {'id': 2, 'text': 'Python is a snake'}]). results = ranker.rerank(req). results[0].score → float. results[0].text, results[0].id. Models: ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2 (default), rank-T5-flan, ms-marco-TinyBERT-L-2-v2, ce-esci-MiniLM-L12-v2. ONNX runtime. No GPU required. Fast enough for real-time re-ranking. 25-100MB model size.

gotchas: Model downloads from HuggingFace on first use — requires internet or pre-cached
Only point-wise re-ranking (not list-wise like RankGPT)
Limited model selection vs FlagEmbedding or cross-encoders from sentence-transformers
No fine-tuning capability — pre-trained models only
best_when: RAG pipeline re-ranking to improve top-k document quality
When cross-encoder quality is needed but full BERT is too slow
CPU-only production deployments needing lightweight re-ranker
avoid_when: When fine-tuned domain-specific re-ranker is needed
When ColBERT (late interaction) is available (use RAGatouille)
List-wise re-ranking with LLM quality (use RankGPT)
summary: FlashRank re-ranks documents for RAG using ONNX-optimized cross-encoders. Ranker('ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2').rerank(RerankRequest(query, passages)). 5-50ms latency. No GPU required. Multiple models (MiniLM, TinyBERT, T5). Apache-2.0. Best for real-time RAG re-ranking on CPU. Model downloads on first use. Point-wise only. No fine-tuning. Outperforms BM25 re-ranking with small compute budget.
